title: LLM decoder floating-point probability format sweep
hypothesis: Floating-point-like decoder formats should preserve large-vocab probability dynamic range differently from fixed integer probability quantization, so a rough fp-format map should be evaluated before optimizing one approximation knob.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Which fp-like formats preserve next-token and top-k quality when applied to logits, softmax intermediates, reciprocal normalization, and final probabilities on the pinned tiny decoder benchmark?
include: exact softmax and normalization reference candidate
fp16, bf16, fp8_e5m2, and fp8_e4m3 logit probes
fp16 and bf16 softmax input/weight probes
fp16, bf16, fp8_e5m2, and fp8_e4m3 final probability probes
per-sample next-token and top-k miss lists
exclude: selecting a final hardware floating-point format
follow_on_broad_sweep: expand prompt/model/logit-margin distributions before promoting any fp approximation format

expected_benefit: separates fixed-point probability underflow from fp-like dynamic-range behavior
maps whether fp-like final probability storage preserves rank where unsigned q8 collapses
keeps the distribution-dependence caveat explicit before later hardware work
risks: the tiny prompt/model setup is too narrow to predict broader LLM behavior
software-emulated fp-like quantization may not exactly match a later hardware implementation
points that preserve rank here may fail on different weight or input distributions
